weaknesses: No explicit Redshift or Snowflake-specific admin details
improvement_bullets: Developed and deployed a production-grade ELT pipeline ingesting Federal Reserve economic data into Google BigQuery using Python, Airflow, and GCS, with automated data quality validation via Great Expectations and CI/CD integration through GitHub Actions.

Architected and implemented a dimensional data model (star schema) in BigQuery for economic data, optimizing analyst query performance through partitioning and clustering, reducing query times for stakeholders.
Leveraged agentic systems principles in the development of automated data quality checks and CI/CD pipelines for data infrastructure, ensuring robust and efficient data processing.

Collaborated with product and engineering teams to translate user feedback and business requirements into production code, specifically extracting and transforming data from REST APIs and relational databases to support front-end feature development.
Actively participated in code reviews and contributed to the improvement of data engineering best practices, including unit testing and documentation, within agile development environments.
cover_letter:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am excited to submit my application for the Data Engineer opportunity with Shields Group Search. I am interested in this opportunity because Shields Group Search's mission to build modern tools for modern clients in private banking and wealth infrastructure aligns with my passion for leveraging data to drive transparency and efficiency in complex financial markets. What interests me about working for Shields Group Search is its innovative approach to democratizing access to historically opaque markets, which truly excites and inspires me because I believe data engineering plays a pivotal role in achieving this.

I believe my advanced Python and SQL expertise, coupled with my hands-on experience with cloud data warehousing platforms like BigQuery and Snowflake, would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on building robust, scalable, and efficient data systems. In fact, in my FRED Economic Batch Pipeline project, I designed and built a production-grade ELT pipeline ingesting Federal Reserve economic data into Google BigQuery using Python, Airflow, and GCS. This involved modeling raw API data into a dbt star schema, automating data quality validation with Great Expectations, and deploying a CI/CD pipeline via GitHub Actions. As a result, I successfully created a reliable data foundation that reduced analyst query time and ensured data integrity.

Ultimately, I know that the Data Engineer role at Shields Group Search would allow me to bring my expertise in designing and building scalable ETL/ELT pipelines to ingest, transform, and load data from multiple sources. Additionally, my experience as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my collaborative and problem-solving skills. I worked closely with engineers, product managers, and business stakeholders to translate user feedback into production code, driving a 15% increase in feature adoption. I also extracted and transformed data from REST APIs and relational databases, building structured, clean datasets that directly supported front-end feature development and reduced UI bugs.

I'm excited by the opportunity to use my skills in Python and SQL for data pipeline development and analysis in working on the world-class data infrastructure that supports the modernization of private banking at Shields Group Search. I know that my strong Python and SQL skills, combined with my experience in cloud data warehousing and CI/CD, align with what it takes to succeed in the Data Engineer role. I am also eager to apply my developing understanding of agentic systems principles to practical, hands-on data engineering challenges. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail.

Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Warm regards,
Justin Miller
